title: Jumps Requested vs. Completed
query: SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM CarrierJumpRequest WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate) AS requested, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM CarrierJump WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate) AS completed

details.text: {requested} jump(s) requested and {completed} completed during the report period. Cancelled jumps are the fingerprints of changed plans — too many cancellations compared to completions suggests persistent obstacles to your fleet mobility or an indecisive period of carrier operations.
details.help: CarrierJumpRequest fires at request time; CarrierJump at completion. requested drives the title as the total mobilisation count.

tagLines: Carrier jump request audit compiled. Request-to-completion ratio calculated for the operations review.
Planning a carrier jump is the easy part. Following through is where the preparation actually mattered.
Cancelled jumps happen. Plans change, targets close, the price of tritium spikes. The log remembers all of it.
